meaning: n.[蟶檎･枉 莉｣霎ｾ鄂玲民
examples: After that, Daedalus was idle no longer.
Daedalus and his son Icarus were prisoners on the island of Crete.
But ever after he mourned his son, and never again did Daedalus try to fly.
Things didn't turn out so well for the young inventor--Daedalus became jealous of his talent, and threw him off the top of the Acropolis.
According to an ancient Greek legend, the first saw was made by a boy named Perdix, who was nephew of the famous inventor Daedalus.
